summary: Matched several Kraid stage functions in src/melee/gr/grkraid.c by making expression-shape, signedness, literal-initialization, and helper-extraction changes, with small header/type cleanup. decomp.dev reported 3 new full matches in main/melee/gr/grkraid: grKraid_801FE440, grKraid_801FEA00, and grKraid_801FEE54, plus improvements to grKraid_801FE6D8 and grKraid_801FE818.

key_files.role: Main matching work for Kraid stage logic; rewrote control-flow/expression forms around map rotation, Kraid attack state machine, random timing, camera calls, and JObj transforms.
Ground variable struct adjustments, notably signedness changes for grKraid_GroundVars.x0 and grKraid_GroundVars2.x3 plus formatting cleanup for unrelated Old Yoshi bitfields/array syntax.

what_changed: Removed the global zero Vec grKr_803B8278 usage in grKraid_801FE440 and used a local Vec initializer { 0.0f, 0.0f, 0.0f } before Camera_80030E44.
Changed grKraid_801FE440 condition style from explicit float comparison to truthiness for gp->gv.kraid.x4, with an in-code remark that explicit != 0.0f led to incorrect ordering.
Compressed absolute-value and selection expressions into ternaries where that matched better, including gp->gv.kraid.xC absolute value and the HSD_Randi(2) choice in grKraid_801FEE54.
Introduced a static helper getMapRotation() for the shared map rotation scalar expression used by grKraid_801FE6D8.
Reworked grKraid_801FE6D8 so hand position X is converted to absolute value inside the rotation assignment expression rather than by mutating handpos.x first.
Added explicit s32 casts around grKr_804D6A08->kraid_wait_time_add nonzero checks before passing possible random wait additions to HSD_Randi.

Reformatted and reshaped the grKraid_801FEA00 state machine without changing the apparent stage-event sequence: animation completion checks, Ground_801C53EC sound/event IDs, JObj flag changes, and hand-triggered map rotation windows remain visible.
Changed grKraid_GroundVars.x0 from u8 to s8 and grKraid_GroundVars2.x3 from u8 to s8 in types.h.
Normalized include style/order in grkraid.c, including moving cm/camera.h earlier and using angle brackets for baselib/random.h.
smart_moves: Used a local zero Vec initializer instead of a named const global to influence data/reference generation while keeping the camera call semantics obvious.
Preserved a matching-sensitive note directly beside the gp->gv.kraid.x4 truthiness check, documenting that a seemingly equivalent explicit float comparison changes instruction ordering.
Factored getMapRotation() out as a static helper, likely guiding the compiler into the desired expression/codegen shape while making the rotation calculation more readable.
Matched signed comparison behavior by correcting ground-var fields from unsigned to signed where state/position selection variables are treated as signed bytes.
Used explicit casts in random-wait ternaries to force the intended comparison/type promotion behavior rather than relying on implicit conversion.
Kept function boundaries and existing symbolic names intact while doing minimal localized expression rewrites, which is ideal for incremental stage matching.
decomp_lessons: For Melee decomp matching, semantically equivalent float checks can produce different instruction ordering; try truthiness versus explicit != 0.0f when a float/state field blocks a match.
Local aggregate initialization can be a useful tactic when a global constant Vec creates the wrong reference or data layout for a function match.
Signedness in GroundVars structs matters even for byte-sized state fields; u8 versus s8 can affect comparisons, casts, and register extension.
Small helper functions can be matching tools, not just readability tools, especially when isolating arithmetic involving global parameter tables and float constants.
Ternary forms can better express compiler-selected abs/select idioms than expanded if/else assignments.
When a config field is later used as an integer random bound, explicit casts in the nonzero test may be necessary to reproduce original codegen.
naming_conventions: Stage-specific functions follow the existing grKraid_801Fxxxx address-based naming convention.
Global Kraid parameter data uses grKr_804D6A08 and typed grKr_804D6A08_t naming.
Ground variable members remain placeholder offset-style names such as x0, x1, x4, x8, xC, x10 while matching is still in progress.
The new local helper uses lowerCamelCase getMapRotation(), unlike address-based recovered functions, indicating it is an internal decomp convenience helper rather than a recovered symbol.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace explicit `gp->gv.kraid.x4 != 0.0f` with `if (gp->gv.kraid.x4)` when float comparison ordering differs.
Use `Vec pos = { 0.0f, 0.0f, 0.0f };` locally for zero-vector camera calls instead of referencing a const global.
Prefer ternary absolute-value expressions such as `x < 0.0f ? -x : x` when they generate the desired instruction sequence.
Extract arithmetic such as `((max / min) / 132.0f) * min` into a static helper to influence surrounding codegen in grKraid_801FE6D8.
Cast `kraid_wait_time_add` to s32 in ternary conditions before HSD_Randi to match integer comparison behavior.
Correct struct field signedness to match lbz/extsb or signed-byte comparison patterns.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report showed matched code increased by 0.06% (+2300 bytes), with 3 new grkraid matches and 2 unmatched-item improvements.
No human review comments or review feedback were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find other stage PRs where float truthiness matched better than explicit != 0.0f comparisons.
Search for GroundVars byte fields still typed u8 but used as signed state or index values.
Look for other camera calls using global zero Vec constants that might match better with local Vec initializers.
Compare grKraid_801FE6D8 after this PR to see what remains for the final 0.40% unmatched portion.
Search for helper-extraction tactics similar to getMapRotation in other gr stage files.
